content:
**Problem restatement:**

A box has 5 chips: 3 red (R) and 2 white (W). Chips are removed one at a time without replacement until **all chips of one color are drawn** (i.e., until either all 3 red chips are drawn or all 2 white chips are drawn).

We want the probability that the **last chip drawn** (the chip that causes the stopping condition) is white.

---

**Key points:**

- The process stops as soon as all chips of one color are drawn.
- The last chip drawn is the one that completes the set of either all red or all white chips.
- We want \( P(\text{last chip drawn is white}) \).

---

**Step 1: Understand stopping condition**

- The process stops at the earliest step \( n \) where all 3 red chips or all 2 white chips have been drawn.
- The last chip drawn is either the 3rd red chip (if reds are completed first) or the 2nd white chip (if whites are completed first).

---

**Step 2: Possible stopping times**

- To get all white chips (2 whites), we must have drawn at least 2 chips.
- To get all red chips (3 reds), we must have drawn at least 3 chips.

Since the box has 5 chips total, the stopping time can be from 2 up to 5.
